prompt: In the flutter-intellij plugin, I'm seeing that when I run a Flutter Windows desktop app in debug mode, exceptions thrown by the Flutter framework are not appearing in the IntelliJ console. The same app works fine on Chrome. What's the code path responsible for showing these errors, and where is the most likely failure point on Windows? Walk me through the relevant classes and show where you'd add a fix.

assertions.text: Mentions ext.flutter.inspector.structuredErrors or structured errors being enabled as the reason Chrome differs from Windows
Mentions FlutterConsoleLogManager or handleFlutterErrorEvent as the handler for Flutter.Error events
Identifies the silent exception catch (try/catch that only logs a warning) in processFlutterErrorEvent as the failure point
Proposes a concrete fix — either a fallback to display raw error text on exception, or adds error visibility when processFlutterErrorEvent throws
Explains why Chrome works differently (extension not available on web, so app.log path is used instead)
